A theming release: Krusader's KDE Breeze Dark scheme is replaced by a new monochrome **Dark Mode** (IBM Carbon greyscale), the WebUI now opens over HTTPS so the browser clipboard works, and the container announces when it is ready in the log.

## Added
- **Dark Mode colour scheme** — a monochrome IBM-Carbon greyscale theme (base `#161616`, **no colour accent**; selection/focus is a light grey `#525252`; the only non-grey tones are the functional error/warning colours `#fa4d56` / `#f1c21b`). Replaces the previous KDE Breeze Dark scheme across Krusader, Kate and the whole KDE stack.
- **Custom Kate "Dark Mode" theme** — a matching, near-monochrome editor/syntax theme so Kate fits the new look.
- **READY log banner** — the container now prints a loud `KRUSADER IS READY` banner to the log once the WebUI is serving, so you know the (multi-second) first start has finished.

## Changed
- **The WebUI now opens over HTTPS (port 3001) by default** — browsers only allow seamless clipboard sync over HTTPS. HTTP (port 3000) stays as an optional fallback. Accept the self-signed certificate once.
- **Top toolbar/menu background lightened** from near-black to the Carbon panel tone (`#262626`).
- Removed the unused `TITLE` template variable.

## Fixed
- **Double init banner** — the LinuxServer base image printed its own brand banner on top of ours. The base banner is now emptied, so the log shows a single, clean banner with proper spacing before the title block.

## Notes
- The bottom KDE status bar cannot be hidden persistently: Krusader 2.8.1 does not honour the config for it, and hiding it cleanly would require an application stylesheet that disables the status bar's custom colouring. The per-panel (upper) status bar and its custom colours are unaffected.
